overview: A simple cloud-native time service exposing a single HTTP API that returns the current server time in JSON format. The architecture consists of a lightweight backend service that handles GET requests and responds with ISO 8601 formatted timestamps including timezone information. This service is designed for high availability and can be consumed by any HTTP client.
requirements: The service must expose an HTTP GET endpoint that returns the current server time
The response must be in JSON format
The time must be formatted according to ISO 8601 standard
The response must include timezone information
The service must be accessible via a predictable URL path
The service must be available for consumption by any HTTP client or browser
The service must include a health check endpoint for monitoring

components.name: time-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Time Service API
  description: A service that returns the current server time in JSON format
  version: 1.0.0
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /time:
    get:
      summary: Get current server time
      description: Returns the current server time in ISO 8601 format with timezone information
      operationId: getCurrentTime
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successfully retrieved current time
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  timestamp:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                    description: Current server time in ISO 8601 format
                    example: '2024-01-15T14:30:45.123Z'
                  timezone:
                    type: string
                    description: Server timezone
                    example: 'UTC'
                required:
                  - timestamp
                  - timezone
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    description: Error message
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      description: Returns the health status of the service
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: 'ok'
                required:
                  - status
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go-based HTTP service using the standard net/http package that listens on port 9090. Create two endpoints: 1) GET /time - returns the current server time using time.Now() formatted as ISO 8601 (RFC3339) with timezone information. The response should be JSON with fields 'timestamp' (string in ISO 8601 format) and 'timezone' (string). Use time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) for the timestamp and time.Now().Location().String() for the timezone. 2) GET /health - returns JSON with status 'ok' to indicate service health. Use json.NewEncoder to write responses. Set Content-Type header to 'application/json' for all responses. Handle errors gracefully with proper HTTP status codes (500 for internal errors). The service should be stateless, require no external dependencies, and start immediately when the container runs. Main function should call http.ListenAndServe(':9090', nil) with registered handlers. Include proper error handling for JSON encoding failures. Log startup message and any errors to stdout. The Dockerfile should use a multi-stage build with golang:1.21-alpine as builder and alpine:latest as runtime, exposing port 9090, and running the compiled binary as the entrypoint.
